perturbation.original_text: 4. Time Periods The non-disclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Time Periods The non-disclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until Disclosing Party *verbally* releases Receiving Party from this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.' and modifying the release method to 'verbally,' the duration of confidentiality is significantly shortened and relies solely on the Disclosing Party's verbal statement, making it difficult to prove if a release was given. The removal of the 'trade secret' clause eliminates a legal standard for confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Notice of Breach The Receiving Party shall immediately notify the Disclosing Party upon discovering any unauthorized use of disclosure of Confidential Information by the Receiving Party or its Representatives, or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives, and will cooperate with any efforts by the DIsclosing Party to assist the Disclosing Party to regain the possession of its Confidential Information and thus prevent its further unauthorized use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the conditions under which confidentiality ends. The original text specified confidentiality would end when the information was no longer a trade secret, or upon written notice. The changed text removes the 'trade secret' condition and changes the release method to 'verbally.' This directly contradicts section 7, which requires the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party upon discovering any unauthorized use of disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement. The original clause allowed for an objective measure (trade secret status) to determine unauthorized use. By making release verbal and removing the 'trade secret' clause, it's unclear when the Receiving Party is obligated to report a 'breach,' as verbal release is hard to prove and could be claimed retroactively, thus creating a strong contradiction with the reporting requirements of section 7. This is an Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Remedies Both parties to this Agreement acknowledge and agree that the Confidential Information hereunder this Agreement is of a unique and valuable nature, and that the unauthorized distribution and broadcasting of the Confidential Information could have the potential to destroy and, at the very least, diminish the value of such information.The damages that the Disclosing Party could sustain as a direct result of the unauthorized dissemination of the Confidential Information would be impossible to calculate. Therefore, both parties hereby agree that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to claim injunctive relief that would prevent the dissemination of any Confidential Information that would be in violation of the terms set forth herein this Agreement. Any such injunctive relief provided shall be in addition to any other available remedies hereunder, whether at law or in equity. The Disclosing Party shall be entitled to recover any sustained costs and/or fees, including, but not limited to, any reasonable attorney’s fees which may be incurred while attempting to obtain any such relief. Furthermore, in the event of any litigation which may be related to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover any such reasonable attorney’s fees and expenses incurred.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Remedies Both parties to this Agreement acknowledge that the Confidential Information hereunder this Agreement is of a nature, and that the unauthorized distribution of the Confidential Information could have the potential to diminish the value of such information. The damages that the Disclosing Party could sustain as a direct result of the unauthorized dissemination of the Confidential Information would be difficult to calculate. Therefore, both parties hereby agree that the Disclosing Party may claim injunctive relief that would prevent the dissemination of any Confidential Information. Any such injunctive relief provided shall be in addition to other available remedies hereunder. The Disclosing Party shall be entitled to recover sustained costs.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrases 'unique and valuable', 'and broadcasting', 'at the very least', 'impossible to calculate', 'in violation of the terms set forth herein this Agreement', 'whether at law or in equity', 'and/or fees, including, but not limited to, any reasonable attorney’s fees which may be incurred while attempting to obtain any such relief', 'Furthermore, in the event of any litigation which may be related to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover any such reasonable attorney’s fees and expenses incurred.' makes the remedies section less enforceable. Changing 'shall' to 'may' regarding the claim to injunctive relief reduces the guaranteed availability of this remedy. Also, removing 'fees' from the cost recovery clause means that the attorney fees will not be paid in court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Obligations of Receiving Party 3.1 Receiving Party shall endeavor to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in confidence for the benefit of the Disclosing Party. 3.2 Receiving Party shall restrict access to Confidential Information to employees and contractors. 3.3 Receiving Party shall not, without approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. 3.4 Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Information immediately if Disclosing Party requests it.
perturbation.justification: The original text guaranteed certain remedies for the Disclosing Party, including injunctive relief ('shall be entitled to claim') and recovery of attorney's fees in litigation. The changed text weakens these guarantees by changing 'shall' to 'may' regarding injunctive relief and removing the entitlement to recover attorney's fees. This creates a contradiction with Section 3, which outlines the Receiving Party's obligations to protect Confidential Information. Because the remedies are weakened, the enforcement of these obligations becomes less certain, creating a strong inconsistency. The omission of guaranteed attorney's fees directly impacts the Disclosing Party's ability to enforce Receiving Party's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Notice of Breach The Receiving Party shall immediately notify the Disclosing Party upon discovering any unauthorized use of disclosure of Confidential Information by the Receiving Party or its Representatives, or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives, and will cooperate with any efforts by the DIsclosing Party to assist the Disclosing Party to regain the possession of its Confidential Information and thus prevent its further unauthorized use.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Notice of Breach The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party upon discovering any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by the Receiving Party, and will cooperate with any efforts by the Disclosing Party to assist the Disclosing Party to regain the possession of its Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'immediately' weakens the urgency of notification, and removing 'use' of Confidential Information removes liability for use. Also, by removing 'or its Representatives, or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives, and thus prevent its further unauthorized use', limits the scope of notification to only unauthorized disclosures by the Receiving Party themselves.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Remedies Both parties to this Agreement acknowledge that the Confidential Information hereunder this Agreement is of a nature, and that the unauthorized distribution of the Confidential Information could have the potential to diminish the value of such information. The damages that the Disclosing Party could sustain as a direct result of the unauthorized dissemination of the Confidential Information would be difficult to calculate. Therefore, both parties hereby agree that the Disclosing Party may claim injunctive relief that would prevent the dissemination of any Confidential Information. Any such injunctive relief provided shall be in addition to other available remedies hereunder. The Disclosing Party shall be entitled to recover sustained costs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the word 'immediately', weakening the notification requirement, and removes 'use' of confidential information, removing liability for unauthorized use. Also, it removes 'or its Representatives, or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives, and thus prevent its further unauthorized use', limiting the scope of notification only to unauthorized disclosures. This directly contradicts section 6, which provides remedies for 'unauthorized dissemination of the Confidential Information' and does not limit liability to only disclosures by the Receiving Party themselves, but also includes 'use' of confidential information by representatives. The original text's broader scope aligns with the remedies, while the changed text narrows the notification obligation, creating a contradiction because now certain breaches for which remedies exist might not be reported.
